info.name: Nalam App - Complete API Collection
info.description:
Complete API collection for Nalam healthcare mobile app. Covers all 5 roles: Patient, Doctor, Receptionist, Pharmacist, Admin.

**How Authentication & Hospital Scoping Works:**
- The JWT token from verify-otp contains embedded claims: userId, role, hospitalId, fullName
- Every API call sends this token via `Authorization: Bearer <token>`
- The backend extracts hospitalId FROM the token (not from headers/params)
- All database queries are automatically filtered by hospitalId via EF Core global query filters + PostgreSQL RLS
- You do NOT need to pass hospitalId separately — it travels inside the JWT

**Setup:**
1. Set `baseUrl` variable to your API URL
2. Run the Auth folder first to get tokens (tokens + hospitalId auto-saved)
3. All subsequent requests use the saved token which carries hospitalId internally

variable.description: Auto-extracted from verify-otp response. This same value is embedded in the JWT token and used by the backend for tenant scoping.

item.name: 1. Authentication
item.description: OTP-based authentication for all user roles. No prior token needed.

request.description: Send OTP to a mobile number. Works for all user roles. Rate-limited (429 if too many requests).

response.name: Rate Limited

request.description: Verify OTP and receive JWT token. Token is auto-saved as patientToken variable.

request.description: Self-registration for new patients. No token required.

item.name: 2. Patient - Appointments
item.description: Patient appointment booking, listing, rescheduling, and cancellation.

request.description: Get paginated list of available doctors. Results are automatically filtered to the patient's hospital (extracted from JWT hospitalId claim). No separate hospitalId parameter needed. Supports filtering by specialty and name search.

response.name: Success
response.body:
{
  "total": 15,
  "page": 1,
  "pageSize": 10,
  "doctors": [
    {
      "doctorProfileId": "dp_001",
      "userId": "usr_doctor_001",
      "fullName": "Dr. Aravind Kumar",
      "initials": "AK",
      "specialty": "Cardiology",
      "department": "Heart Care",
      "experienceYears": 12,
      "consultationFee": 500,
      "availableForVideo": true,
      "availableForInPerson": true,
      "languages": "English, Tamil",
      "rating": 4.5,
      "reviewCount": 120,
      "profilePhotoUrl": null,
      "isAcceptingAppointments": true
    }
  ]
}

request.description: Get available time slots for a specific doctor over a date range.

body.raw:
{
  "doctorProfileId": "{{doctorProfileId}}",
  "scheduleDate": "2026-03-25",
  "startTime": "09:00",
  "consultationType": "video",
  "notes": "Chest pain and breathlessness for past 3 days"
}

request.description: Book a new appointment. consultationType: 'video' or 'in-person'. Hospital scoping: the appointment is automatically linked to the patient's hospitalId (from JWT token). The doctorProfileId must belong to the same hospital (enforced by EF Core query filters).

response.name: Success
response.body:
{
  "id": "apt_20260325_001",

item.name: 10. Error & Edge Cases
item.description: Tests for authentication errors, validation, and edge cases.

request.description: Should return 401 when no auth token provided.
Send OTP multiple times rapidly to trigger 429 rate limiting.
Attempt to book with an invalid date/time — should return 400.
